results.justification: Both domain rules with testable assertions, all 3 architecture decisions with implementation notes, ORDER_STATE_INVALID preserved verbatim, terminology_alignment present (3 entries), task_context specific and actionable, no invented information.

Open questions section identifies gaps without inventing answers

results.agent_output_excerpt:
handoff_brief:
  recipient: Shaq

  task_context: >
    You are implementing the order state transition feature within the Order
    Fulfillment bounded context. This involves building a PATCH endpoint that
    accepts state change requests for orders, validating those transitions
    against a state machine, and enforcing two hard invariants: (1) orders
    cannot revert from SHIPPED back to PENDING, and (2) refunds can only be
    issued against orders in DELIVERED state.

results.justification: Contradiction explicitly named with both agents' exact positions quoted. Escalated to Coach K (not Shaq). Asked the right question about precondition vs exception window. 3 concrete resolution options (domain wins, reclassify invariant, new intermediate state). No brief produced. Not routed to Shaq.

observations.text: Explained WHY each of 3 possible Shaq paths would be wrong without resolution
Offered to produce partial brief for non-conflicting parts but did not produce it

results.agent_output_excerpt:
ESCALATION — Contradiction Detected Before Synthesis

From: Magic Johnson (Context Synthesizer)
To: Coach K (and team)
Re: Bird vs. MJ — Irreconcilable conflict on IN_TRANSIT courier assignment invariant

The Conflict

Bird (Domain Rule — invariant: true)
> A courier must be assigned to a delivery before the delivery enters IN_TRANSIT state.

MJ (Architecture Decision)
> The delivery state machine will allow transitioning to IN_TRANSIT without a courier assignment
